title: Tephrosia cinerea
intro: Tephrosia cinerea, also known as Grey Horsefly Tree or Silver-leaf Horsefly Tree, is a perennial legume native to tropical Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tephrosia cinerea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tephrosia cinerea is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used as a paste for topical applications, and is also incorporated into herbal teas and tinctures.
taxonomy: Tephrosia cinerea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae, within the genus Tephrosia. The genus Tephrosia is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. Tephrosia cinerea is a part of the tribe Millettieae, which includes around 220 species. The classification of Tephrosia cinerea is further supported by its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and diversity within the genus Tephrosia.
